sections.title: Will / Shall — Introduction and Formula
sections.explanation: Will / Shall is an essential building block of spoken and written English. Formula: Subject + will/shall + base verb (future actions, offers, promises). Rules: 'Will' is used for future actions, decisions, promises with all subjects | 'Shall' is more formal, traditionally used with I/We for offers and suggestions | Negative: will not / won't, shall not | Question: Will/Shall + subject + base verb...?
sections.formula: Subject + will/shall + base verb (future actions, offers, promises)

examples.hindi: क्या मैं दरवाज़ा बंद कर दूँ?

sections.title: Will / Shall — Daily Life Usage
sections.explanation: Everyday sentences using Will / Shall for daily conversation.
sections.formula: Subject + will/shall + base verb (future actions, offers, promises)

examples.english: I will not break this promise.
She will become a doctor in the future.

sections.title: Will / Shall — Office & Professional Usage
sections.hindiTitle: Will / Shall — ऑफिस और पेशेवर उपयोग
sections.explanation: Practice Will / Shall in professional and office contexts.
sections.formula: Subject + will/shall + base verb (future actions, offers, promises)

examples.hindi: मैं कल यह काम खत्म कर दूँगा।
examples.english: I will finish this work tomorrow.

sections.title: Will / Shall — Negative and Question Forms
sections.hindiTitle: Will / Shall — नकारात्मक और प्रश्न रूप
sections.explanation: How to make negative sentences and questions using Will / Shall. Common mistakes: wrong verb form after the modal, or missing "have" in perfect modals.
sections.formula: Subject + will/shall + base verb (future actions, offers, promises)

examples.english: He will get married next year.

examples.hindi: मैं यह गलती फिर नहीं करूँगा।
examples.english: I will not make this mistake again.
